title: Early theories of mind: what the theory theory can tell us about autism
abstract: Abstract In the first edition of this book we presented a hypothesis about the early development of children’s understanding of the mind (Meltzoff and Gopnik 1993).We suggested that normally-developing children are born linking their own minds and the minds of others. This ability is most clearly evidenced in the innate capacity to imitate facial expressions. This primitive link means that, later on in development, further discoveries about other people’s minds are easily and naturally extended to knowledge of our own minds, and vice versa. We suggested that this initial link between the self and others may be missing in at least some cases of autism.
